steps.title: Apply the vertical translation rule
steps.content:
Translating a graph up $k$ units transforms the function by adding $k$ to its value. Since the graph of $y = g(x)$ is the result of translating $y = f(x)$ up 5 units, we have:
$$g(x) = f(x) + 5$$
To find $g(0)$, we need:
$$g(0) = f(0) + 5$$

steps.title: Calculate the product and find g(0)
steps.content:
Multiply the factors step by step:
$$(-7)(-4) = 28$$
$$28 \cdot (-2) = -56$$
So $f(0) = -56$. Therefore:
$$g(0) = f(0) + 5 = -56 + 5 = -51$$
